frameworks.name: locus coeruleus-norepinephrine arousal
frameworks.one_line: pupil size as readout of arousal/effort modulating tonic and phasic responses

frameworks.name: covert spatial attention
frameworks.one_line: attentional breadth (foveal vs peripheral) as distinct modulator of pupil baseline

core_question: Do task difficulty and attentional breadth modulate tonic baseline pupil size and phasic stimulus-locked pupil response through shared or separate mechanisms?
key_assumptions: tonic baseline and phasic stimulus-response are dissociable pupil signals
covert attentional breadth can be parametrically allocated to foveal vs peripheral locations
manipulations: attentional breadth (foveal vs peripheral covert attention)
key_findings: higher task difficulty increased both tonic baseline and phasic response amplitude
broader peripheral attention increased only tonic baseline, not phasic response
interpretations: tonic and phasic pupil modulation rely on at least partly separate mechanisms
task-difficulty engages both systems whereas attentional-breadth engages only the tonic system
